textPlain: Slightly off topic but chatGPT’s refusal to visually identify people, including dead historical personalities, has been a big let down for me. I can paste in an image of JFK and it will refuse to tell me who it is.
Why do so many bad blog posts get second chances these day? They are also mostly AI related?
Simon's writing is consistently either highly practical, or extremely high quality, or both. What's your reference frame to call it "bad" - your own comments?
